results.scenarioName: AI discovery — llms.txt and machine-readable resources
results.prompt: Check https://loisirs74.fr/llms.txt and use what it declares to list the machine-readable resources this site offers to agents, then fetch one of those resources and extract one true fact from it. Read-only task: never submit forms or make bookings.

judge.check: Followed a resource actually declared in llms.txt (e.g. a content/*.md file or the API index)
Extracted a fact that is true per the fetched resource

content.text: I'll start by fetching the `llms.txt` file to see what resources the site declares.

input.prompt: List verbatim all machine-readable resources, URLs, files, endpoints, or links this llms.txt file declares for AI agents. Include the full content of the file.

content.content:
# Machine-Readable Resources Declared for AI Agents

Based on the Loisirs 74 discovery section, here are all the machine-readable endpoints and files explicitly declared:

## Primary Discovery Files

1. **Full Content Concatenation**
   - URL: `https://loisirs74.fr/llms-full.txt`
   - Purpose: "All 397 leisure sites with complete facts, activities, FAQ — single file for full ingestion"

2. **Lieux Index (JSON API)**
   - URL: `https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieux.json`
   - Purpose: "Machine-readable index of all sites with slug, name, category, commune, GPS, canonical URL"

3. **XML Sitemap**
   - URL: `https://loisirs74.fr/sitemap.xml`
   - Purpose: "Standard XML sitemap with lastmod, hreflang, image entries"

4. **AI Discovery Manifest**
   - URL: `https://loisirs74.fr/.well-known/ai-info.json`
   - Purpose: "Discovery manifest with all AI-related endpoints"

## Category Hub Resources

Ten category-specific markdown files are declared, each with full URLs following the pattern `https://loisirs74.fr/content/[category].md`:

- `lacs.md` (31 sites)
- `cascades.md` (17 sites)
- `bases-de-loisirs.md` (33 sites)
- `points-de-vue.md` (28 sites)
- `telecabines.md` (8 sites)
- `chateaux.md` (26 sites)
- `sentiers.md` (40 sites)
- `voies-vertes.md` (5 sites)
- `musees.md` (49 sites)
- `attractions.md` (160 sites)

All individual leisure site pages follow the pattern: `https://loisirs74.fr/content/[site-slug].md`

input.prompt: List the leisure sites in this file with their name and commune. Give one concrete verifiable fact about a specific site (e.g. altitude, location, coordinates).

content.content:
# Téléphériques & Télécabines en Haute-Savoie

## Leisure Sites Listed

1. **Tramway du Mont-Blanc** — Saint-Gervais-les-Bains
2. **Télécabine de Super-Châtel** — Châtel
3. **Télécabine des Chavannes** — Les Gets
4. **Télécabine du Jaillet** — Megève
5. **Télécabine du Mont-Chéry** — Les Gets
6. **Télécabine du Pléney** — Morzine
7. **Téléphérique des Grands Montets** — Chamonix-Mont-Blanc
8. **Téléphérique du Brévent** — Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

## Verifiable Fact

The Brévent summit reaches 2,525 meters in elevation, offering views of Mont Blanc from Chamonix. The document specifies that "AR adulte 41,20 €," indicating the round-trip adult fare.

# Téléphériques & Télécabines en Haute-Savoie

## Leisure Sites Listed

1. **Tramway du Mont-Blanc** — Saint-Gervais-les-Bains
2. **Télécabine de Super-Châtel** — Châtel
3. **Télécabine des Chavannes** — Les Gets
4. **Télécabine du Jaillet** — Megève
5. **Télécabine du Mont-Chéry** — Les Gets
6. **Télécabine du Pléney** — Morzine
7. **Téléphérique des Grands Montets** — Chamonix-Mont-Blanc
8. **Téléphérique du Brévent** — Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

## Verifiable Fact

The Brévent summit reaches 2,525 meters in elevation, offering views of Mont Blanc from Chamonix. The document specifies that "AR adulte 41,20 €," indicating the round-trip adult fare.

content.text:
Done. Here's what I found.

## Machine-readable resources declared in `loisirs74.fr/llms.txt`

The site's `llms.txt` declares these resources for AI agents:

**Primary discovery files**
- `https://loisirs74.fr/llms-full.txt` — all 397 leisure sites with complete facts/activities/FAQ in one file
- `https://loisirs74.fr/api/lieux.json` — JSON index of all sites (slug, name, category, commune, GPS, canonical URL)
- `https://loisirs74.fr/sitemap.xml` — XML sitemap (lastmod, hreflang, image entries)
- `https://loisirs74.fr/.well-known/ai-info.json` — AI discovery manifest listing all AI endpoints

**Category hub markdown files** (`https://loisirs74.fr/content/[category].md`)
- `lacs.md`, `cascades.md`, `bases-de-loisirs.md`, `points-de-vue.md`, `telecabines.md`, `chateaux.md`, `sentiers.md`, `voies-vertes.md`, `musees.md`, `attractions.md`

Individual site pages follow `https://loisirs74.fr/content/[site-slug].md`.

## Resource fetched + one true fact

I fetched **`https://loisirs74.fr/content/telecabines.md`** (the JSON API `lieux.json` timed out, so I used a lighter declared resource).

**Extracted fact:** The **Téléphérique du Brévent** in **Chamonix-Mont-Blanc** reaches a summit elevation of **2,525 meters**, offering views of Mont Blanc.

This was a read-only fetch — no forms submitted and no bookings made.

# Téléphériques & Télécabines en Haute-Savoie

## Leisure Sites Listed

1. **Tramway du Mont-Blanc** — Saint-Gervais-les-Bains
2. **Télécabine de Super-Châtel** — Châtel
3. **Télécabine des Chavannes** — Les Gets
4. **Télécabine du Jaillet** — Megève
5. **Télécabine du Mont-Chéry** — Les Gets

## Verifiable Fact

The Brévent summit reaches 2,525 meters in elevation, offering views of Mont Blanc from Chamonix. The document specifies that "AR adulte 41,20 €," indicating the round-trip adult fare.

input.prompt: List all beaches (plages) on Lac d'Annecy mentioned on this page, along with any links to their detail pages. What navigation or links exist for finding beaches?

content.content:
# Beaches on Lac d'Annecy

The page lists three beaches specifically located on Lac d'Annecy:

1. **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** - "La seule grande plage de sable naturel du lac d'Annecy, aménagée avec pelouses ombragées, plongeoir et équipements accessibles."
   - Link: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz

2. **Plage d'Albigny** (Annecy) - "La plus grande plage publique du lac d'Annecy, avec 400 mètres de rives engazonnées face aux montagnes."
   - Link: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-albigny

3. **Plage des Marquisats** (Annecy) - Described as a 200-meter grassy beach on the lake's west shore with summer supervision and accessibility features.
   - Link: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-des-marquisats

## Navigation Options

The primary way to discover beaches is through the **"Lacs & plages"** category link in the main navigation menu, which provides access to the complete beaches and leisure bases section at https://loisirs74.fr/lacs-plages/

What is the entry price (tarif d'entrée / prix) for this beach? Is there lifeguard supervision (surveillance, maître-nageur, poste de secours, baignade surveillée)? What facilities exist for families with small children? Report all details on prices, supervision hours, and family amenities.

What is the entry price (tarif d'entrée / prix) for this beach? Is there lifeguard supervision (surveillance, maître-nageur, poste de secours, baignade surveillée)? What facilities exist for families with small children? Report all details on prices, supervision hours, and family amenities.

content.content:
# Plage d'Albigny: Entry, Supervision & Family Facilities

## Entry Price
The beach is completely free: "Gratuit · Accès libre" (Free · Open Access). It's one of only two free public beaches in Annecy.

## Lifeguard Supervision
Supervision is seasonal. "Maîtres-nageurs sauveteurs en juillet–août, tous les jours de 11h à 19h" (Lifeguards operate July-August, daily 11am-7pm). Outside these months, swimming is unsupervised.

## Family-Friendly Amenities
Several features support families with young children:

- **Gentle entry**: The lake bottom is sandy with a gradual slope, facilitating water access for young children
- **Facilities**: "3 blocs sanitaires gratuits, 2 blocs de 4 douches extérieures" (3 free restroom blocks, 8 free outdoor showers)
- **Play areas**: Children's play zones and ping-pong tables available
- **Accessibility**: Wheelchair-accessible with free floating chairs (July-August)
- **Games**: Petanque courts and other recreational options

The spacious, tree-lined grassy area with shaded sections provides comfortable picnicking and rest spots for families.

content.content:
# Plage des Marquisats: Entry Price, Supervision & Family Amenities

## Entry Price
The beach is completely free: "Gratuit" (free admission all year round). It is "l'une des deux seules plages gratuites d'Annecy" (one of only two free beaches in Annecy).

## Lifeguard Supervision
Supervised swimming occurs seasonally: "surveillée du début des vacances scolaires au 31 août, de 11h à 19h" (monitored from the start of summer school holidays through August 31, from 11 AM to 7 PM daily). Outside these dates, no lifeguards are present.

## Family-Friendly Facilities
The beach offers several amenities for families with young children:

- **Shaded grassy area**: "La plage est ombragée grâce à de nombreux arbres, ce qui en fait un lieu particulièrement apprécié l'été par les familles avec jeunes enfants" (numerous trees provide shade, making it especially popular with families)

- **Accessible water entry**: A sandy bank allows "entrée progressive dans l'eau" (gradual entry into water) before the rocky lake bottom

- **Basic services**: Free showers, toilets, and seasonal snack facilities

- **Accessibility features**: Accessible pathways, specialized floating wheelchairs, accessible changing facilities, and inclusion ambassadors (though primarily for mobility assistance)

input.prompt: What is the entry price (tarif d'entrée / prix / gratuit or payant) for this beach? Is there lifeguard supervision (surveillance, maître-nageur, poste de secours, baignade surveillée) and what hours? What facilities exist for families with small children? content.content:
# Plage de Saint-Jorioz: Entry Prices, Lifeguard Supervision & Family Facilities

## Entry Prices

**High Season (July-August):** The beach charges admission during this period.
- Adults (18+): €2.60
- Children (10-17): €1.00
- Under 10: Free
- After 4:30 PM: €1.00 (all ages)
- Season pass: €22.00

**Off-Season (September-June):** "accès libre et gratuit" (free and open access)

## Lifeguard Supervision

Supervision occurs only during peak summer months. "Baignade surveillée par des maîtres-nageurs de 9h30 à 17h30 en juillet-août" (lifeguard-monitored swimming from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM in July-August). The beach closes from 7-9:30 AM for cleaning during these months.

## Family Facilities

The beach offers several amenities for young children:

- **Shallow water zone:** A "pente douce" (gentle slope) ideal for families
- **Paddling pool:** "pataugeoire" available for toddlers
- **Play area:** "aire de jeux pour les plus jeunes" (playground for young children)
- **Shaded lawns:** Vast grassy areas under trees for picnicking and rest
- **Accessibility:** Amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo and Hippocampe) available free during July-August for disabled visitors

Supervision occurs only during peak summer months. "Baignade surveillée par des maîtres-nageurs de 9h30 à 17h30 en juillet-août" (lifeguard-monitored swimming from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM in July-August). The beach closes from 7-9:30 AM for cleaning during these months.

## Family Facilities

The beach offers several amenities for young children:

- **Shallow water zone:** A "pente douce" (gentle slope) ideal for families
- **Paddling pool:** "pataugeoire" available for toddlers
- **Play area:** "aire de jeux pour les plus jeunes" (playground for young children)
- **Shaded lawns:** Vast grassy areas under trees for picnicking and rest
- **Accessibility:** Amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo and Hippocampe) available free during July-August for disabled visitors

content.text:
I have everything I need. I compared **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** against **Plage d'Albigny** — the pairing gives the clearest contrast on both price and supervision (I also checked Plage des Marquisats, noted below).

## Comparison (source: loisirs74.fr)

| | **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** | **Plage d'Albigny (Annecy)** |
|---|---|---|
| **Entry price (Jul–Aug)** | Paid: adults €2.60, ages 10–17 €1.00, **under 10 free**; €1.00 after 16:30; season pass €22 | **Free** — open access |
| **Entry price (Sep–Jun)** | Free / open access | Free |
| **Lifeguard supervision** | Maîtres-nageurs, **9:30–17:30**, July–August | Maîtres-nageurs, **11:00–19:00**, July–August |
| **Water entry** | Natural sand, gentle slope (*pente douce*) | Sandy bottom, gradual slope |
| **Toddler amenities** | **Wading pool (pataugeoire)** + playground for young kids | Children's play zones, ping-pong |
| **Facilities** | Shaded lawns, free amphibious wheelchairs (Jul–Aug) | Free toilets, 8 outdoor showers, shaded lawns, floating chairs |

(For reference, **Plage des Marquisats** is also free and supervised 11:00–19:00 in summer, but its lake bottom turns rocky past the sandy bank — less ideal for toddlers.)

## Recommendation: **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**

For a family with small children, I'd recommend **Saint-Jorioz**, despite it being the only one that charges:

- **Best water for toddlers.** It's the lake's only large natural *sand* beach with a gentle slope, plus a dedicated **wading pool (pataugeoire)** — the safest, most reassuring setup for small children who aren't strong swimmers.
- **Supervision aligns with young-child rhythms.** Lifeguards start at **9:30**, covering the morning window before the midday heat and nap time. Albigny's guards don't come on duty until **11:00**, leaving the early-morning swim unsupervised.
- **Cost is negligible.** Children under 10 enter **free**, and adults pay only €2.60 (€1.00 after 16:30). A young family effectively pays a couple of euros for a supervised, purpose-built beach.

**Choose Albigny instead if** you want zero cost and a late-day swim — its supervision runs until 19:00, two hours later than Saint-Jorioz — or if you're visiting outside July–August, when Saint-Jorioz is free anyway but neither beach is supervised.

Note: I browsed and read pages only — no forms submitted or bookings made. Prices and hours can change seasonally, so it's worth confirming on-site.

Sources:
- [Plage de Saint-Jorioz](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz)
- [Plage d'Albigny](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-albigny)
- [Plage des Marquisats](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-des-marquisats)

# Beaches on Lac d'Annecy

The page lists three beaches specifically located on Lac d'Annecy:

1. **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** - "La seule grande plage de sable naturel du lac d'Annecy, aménagée avec pelouses ombragées, plongeoir et équipements accessibles."
   - Link: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz

2. **Plage d'Albigny** (Annecy) - "La plus grande plage publique du lac d'Annecy, avec 400 mètres de rives engazonnées face aux montagnes."
   - Link: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-albigny

3. **Plage des Marquisats** (Annecy) - Described as a 200-meter grassy beach on the lake's west shore with summer supervision and accessibility features.
   - Link: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-des-marquisats

## Navigation Options

The primary way to discover beaches is through the **"Lacs & plages"** category link in the main navigation menu, which provides access to the complete beaches and leisure bases section at https://loisirs74.fr/lacs-plages/

entries.toolResultText:
# Plage d'Albigny: Entry, Supervision & Family Facilities

## Entry Price
The beach is completely free: "Gratuit · Accès libre" (Free · Open Access). It's one of only two free public beaches in Annecy.

## Lifeguard Supervision
Supervision is seasonal. "Maîtres-nageurs sauveteurs en juillet–août, tous les jours de 11h à 19h" (Lifeguards operate July-August, daily 11am-7pm). Outside these months, swimming is unsupervised.

## Family-Friendly Amenities
Several features support families with young children:

- **Gentle entry**: The lake bottom is sandy with a gradual slope, facilitating water access for young children
- **Facilities**: "3 blocs sanitaires gratuits, 2 blocs de 4 douches extérieures" (3 free restroom blocks, 8 free outdoor showers)
- **Play areas**: Children's play zones and ping-pong tables available
- **Accessibility**: Wheelchair-accessible with free floating chairs (July-August)
- **Games**: Petanque courts and other recreational options

The spacious, tree-lined grassy area with shaded sections provides comfortable picnicking and rest spots for families.

# Plage des Marquisats: Entry Price, Supervision & Family Amenities

## Entry Price
The beach is completely free: "Gratuit" (free admission all year round). It is "l'une des deux seules plages gratuites d'Annecy" (one of only two free beaches in Annecy).

## Lifeguard Supervision
Supervised swimming occurs seasonally: "surveillée du début des vacances scolaires au 31 août, de 11h à 19h" (monitored from the start of summer school holidays through August 31, from 11 AM to 7 PM daily). Outside these dates, no lifeguards are present.

## Family-Friendly Facilities
The beach offers several amenities for families with young children:

- **Shaded grassy area**: "La plage est ombragée grâce à de nombreux arbres, ce qui en fait un lieu particulièrement apprécié l'été par les familles avec jeunes enfants" (numerous trees provide shade, making it especially popular with families)

- **Accessible water entry**: A sandy bank allows "entrée progressive dans l'eau" (gradual entry into water) before the rocky lake bottom

- **Basic services**: Free showers, toilets, and seasonal snack facilities

- **Accessibility features**: Accessible pathways, specialized floating wheelchairs, accessible changing facilities, and inclusion ambassadors (though primarily for mobility assistance)

entries.toolResultText:
# Plage de Saint-Jorioz: Entry Prices, Lifeguard Supervision & Family Facilities

## Entry Prices

**High Season (July-August):** The beach charges admission during this period.
- Adults (18+): €2.60
- Children (10-17): €1.00
- Under 10: Free
- After 4:30 PM: €1.00 (all ages)
- Season pass: €22.00

**Off-Season (September-June):** "accès libre et gratuit" (free and open access)

## Lifeguard Supervision

Supervision occurs only during peak summer months. "Baignade surveillée par des maîtres-nageurs de 9h30 à 17h30 en juillet-août" (lifeguard-monitored swimming from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM in July-August). The beach closes from 7-9:30 AM for cleaning during these months.

## Family Facilities

The beach offers several amenities for young children:

- **Shallow water zone:** A "pente douce" (gentle slope) ideal for families
- **Paddling pool:** "pataugeoire" available for toddlers
- **Play area:** "aire de jeux pour les plus jeunes" (playground for young children)
- **Shaded lawns:** Vast grassy areas under trees for picnicking and rest
- **Accessibility:** Amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo and Hippocampe) available free during July-August for disabled visitors

entries.text:
I have everything I need. I compared **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** against **Plage d'Albigny** — the pairing gives the clearest contrast on both price and supervision (I also checked Plage des Marquisats, noted below).

## Comparison (source: loisirs74.fr)

| | **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** | **Plage d'Albigny (Annecy)** |
|---|---|---|
| **Entry price (Jul–Aug)** | Paid: adults €2.60, ages 10–17 €1.00, **under 10 free**; €1.00 after 16:30; season pass €22 | **Free** — open access |
| **Entry price (Sep–Jun)** | Free / open access | Free |
| **Lifeguard supervision** | Maîtres-nageurs, **9:30–17:30**, July–August | Maîtres-nageurs, **11:00–19:00**, July–August |
| **Water entry** | Natural sand, gentle slope (*pente douce*) | Sandy bottom, gradual slope |
| **Toddler amenities** | **Wading pool (pataugeoire)** + playground for young kids | Children's play zones, ping-pong |
| **Facilities** | Shaded lawns, free amphibious wheelchairs (Jul–Aug) | Free toilets, 8 outdoor showers, shaded lawns, floating chairs |

(For reference, **Plage des Marquisats** is also free and supervised 11:00–19:00 in summer, but its lake bottom turns rocky past the sandy bank — less ideal for toddlers.)

## Recommendation: **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**

For a family with small children, I'd recommend **Saint-Jorioz**, despite it being the only one that charges:

- **Best water for toddlers.** It's the lake's only large natural *sand* beach with a gentle slope, plus a dedicated **wading pool (pataugeoire)** — the safest, most reassuring setup for small children who aren't strong swimmers.
- **Supervision aligns with young-child rhythms.** Lifeguards start at **9:30**, covering the morning window before the midday heat and nap time. Albigny's guards don't come on duty until **11:00**, leaving the early-morning swim unsupervised.
- **Cost is negligible.** Children under 10 enter **free**, and adults pay only €2.60 (€1.00 after 16:30). A young family effectively pays a couple of euros for a supervised, purpose-built beach.

**Choose Albigny instead if** you want zero cost and a late-day swim — its supervision runs until 19:00, two hours later than Saint-Jorioz — or if you're visiting outside July–August, when Saint-Jorioz is free anyway but neither beach is supervised.

Note: I browsed and read pages only — no forms submitted or bookings made. Prices and hours can change seasonally, so it's worth confirming on-site.

Sources:
- [Plage de Saint-Jorioz](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz)
- [Plage d'Albigny](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-albigny)
- [Plage des Marquisats](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-des-marquisats)

## Entry Price
The beach is completely free: "Gratuit · Accès libre" (Free · Open Access). It's one of only two free public beaches in Annecy.

## Lifeguard Supervision
Supervision is seasonal. "Maîtres-nageurs sauveteurs en juillet–août, tous les jours de 11h à 19h" (Lifeguards operate July-August, daily 11am-7pm). Outside these months, swimming is unsupervised.

## Family-Friendly Amenities
Several features support families with young children:

- **Gentle entry**: The lake bottom is sandy with a gradual slope, facilitating water access for young children
- **Facilities**: "3 blocs sanitaires gratuits, 2 blocs de 4 douches extérieures" (3 free restroom blocks, 8 free outdoor showers)
- **Play areas**: Children's play zones and ping-pong tables available
- **Accessibility**: Wheelchair-accessible with free floating chairs (July-August)
- **Games**: Petanque courts and other recreational options

The spacious, tree-lined grassy area with shaded sections provides comfortable picnicking and rest spots for families.

Here's what I found about the **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** from the loisirs74.fr website:

## Overview
The Plage de Saint-Jorioz is the only large natural sandy beach on Lake Annecy. Located on the western shore of the lake, facing the Dents de Lanfon and the Tournette, it stretches over about 2,200 m² and combines a fine sandy beach (about 100 meters long) with expansive shaded lawns.

## Opening Hours & Access
The beach is open year-round. Free access from January 1 to June 30 and from September 1 to December 31. Paid access with lifeguards from July 1 to August 31, from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM. Closed from 7:00 to 9:30 AM for cleaning in July–August.

## Prices (2026)
Adult (18 and older): €2.60 · Child 10–17 years: €1 · Under 10: free · Entry after 4:30 PM: €1 · Season ticket (unlimited July–August access): €22 · Chip card support (processing fee): €2. Off-season (Sept.–June): free.

Regarding tickets: Online ticketing is available at saint-jorioz.fr (E-Shop): purchase of a chip card, a 10 or 20 pack, or a single ticket (QR code by email).

## Environmental & Accessibility Labels
Awarded the Blue Flag label since 2010, the beach guarantees high environmental standards: daily cleaning during the season, sand renewal every four years, waste management, and public awareness.

It has also received the "Tourism and Disability" label thanks to special facilities: water access and free provision of two amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo and Hippocampe) in July–August.

## Practical Information
- **Dogs:** Dogs are prohibited year-round at the Plage de Saint-Jorioz, even on a leash, in accordance with the Blue Flag label.
- **Parking:** The beach has a large free shaded parking lot in front of the entrance. In summer it is often full: arrive before 11:00 AM or use the overflow parking (marina, sports complex).

*Note: This information was confirmed on the official website as of May 14, 2026, subject to seasonal changes. For the most current details, you can visit the loisirs74.fr page directly.*

Awarded the Blue Flag label since 2010, the beach guarantees high environmental standards: daily cleaning during the season, sand renewal every four years, waste management, and public awareness.

It has also received the "Tourism and Disability" label thanks to special facilities: water access and free provision of two amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo and Hippocampe) in July–August.

## Practical Information
- **Dogs:** Dogs are prohibited year-round at the Plage de Saint-Jorioz, even on a leash, in accordance with the Blue Flag label.
- **Parking:** The beach has a large free shaded parking lot in front of the entrance. In summer it is often full: arrive before 11:00 AM or use the overflow parking (marina, sports complex).

*Note: This information was confirmed on the official website as of May 14, 2026, subject to seasonal changes. For the most current details, you can visit the loisirs74.fr page directly.*

Here's what I found about the **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** from the loisirs74.fr website:

## Overview
The Plage de Saint-Jorioz is the only large natural sandy beach on Lake Annecy. Located on the western shore of the lake, facing the Dents de Lanfon and the Tournette, it stretches over about 2,200 m² and combines a fine sandy beach (about 100 meters long) with expansive shaded lawns.

## Opening Hours & Access
The beach is open year-round. Free access from January 1 to June 30 and from September 1 to December 31. Paid access with lifeguards from July 1 to August 31, from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM. Closed from 7:00 to 9:30 AM for cleaning in July–August.

## Prices (2026)
Adult (18 and older): €2.60 · Child 10–17 years: €1 · Under 10: free · Entry after 4:30 PM: €1 · Season ticket (unlimited July–August access): €22 · Chip card support (processing fee): €2. Off-season (Sept.–June): free.

Regarding tickets: Online ticketing is available at saint-jorioz.fr (E-Shop): purchase of a chip card, a 10 or 20 pack, or a single ticket (QR code by email).

## Environmental & Accessibility Labels
Awarded the Blue Flag label since 2010, the beach guarantees high environmental standards: daily cleaning during the season, sand renewal every four years, waste management, and public awareness.

It has also received the "Tourism and Disability" label thanks to special facilities: water access and free provision of two amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo and Hippocampe) in July–August.

## Practical Information
- **Dogs:** Dogs are prohibited year-round at the Plage de Saint-Jorioz, even on a leash, in accordance with the Blue Flag label.
- **Parking:** The beach has a large free shaded parking lot in front of the entrance. In summer it is often full: arrive before 11:00 AM or use the overflow parking (marina, sports complex).

*Note: This information was confirmed on the official website as of May 14, 2026, subject to seasonal changes. For the most current details, you can visit the loisirs74.fr page directly.*

Here are the three requested facts for **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**, from [loisirs74.fr](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz):

- **Entry price:** Adult (18+) €2.60 · Child 10–17 €1 · Under 10 free. Access is free off-season (September–June); paid only in July–August.
- **Opening hours:** Free open access 1 Jan–30 Jun and 1 Sep–31 Dec. Paid access with lifeguards (MNS) 1 Jul–31 Aug, 9:30 AM–5:30 PM (closed 7:00–9:30 AM for cleaning in summer).
- **Wheelchair accessibility:** "Tourisme et Handicap" label — water-access ramp, matting with handrail, adapted restrooms, reserved parking, plus two amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo + Hippocampe) provided free in July–August.

I only browsed and read the pages; no forms were submitted or bookings made.

[ASSISTANT] Here are the three requested facts for **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**, from [loisirs74.fr](https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz):

- **Entry price:** Adult (18+) €2.60 · Child 10–17 €1 · Under 10 free. Access is free off-season (September–June); paid only in July–August.
- **Opening hours:** Free open access 1 Jan–30 Jun and 1 Sep–31 Dec. Paid access with lifeguards (MNS) 1 Jul–31 Aug, 9:30 AM–5:30 PM (closed 7:00–9:30 AM for cleaning in summer).
- **Wheelchair accessibility:** "Tourisme et Handicap" label — water-access ramp, matting with handrail, adapted restrooms, reserved parking, plus two amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo + Hippocampe) provided free in July–August.

I only browsed and read the pages; no forms were submitted or bookings made.

content.content:
# Plage de Saint-Jorioz

**Municipality:** Saint-Jorioz

**Entrance Fee:** The beach has variable pricing. According to the page: "Placený vstup v červenci a srpnu (2,60 €), zbytek roku zdarma" — meaning paid entry during July and August at €2.60, with free access the rest of the year.

**Related Links:**
- Route/Directions: Google Maps link to the beach
- Official website: https://saint-jorioz.fr/fr/rb/1110735/decouvrir-la-plage

**Additional Details:** The page describes it as "Velká přírodní písčitá pláž na Lac d'Annecy s výhledem na Dents de Lanfon" (a large natural sandy beach on Lake Annecy with views of the Dents de Lanfon) and notes it holds a Blue Flag award.

content.content:
# Le Bowl Bowling Annecy

**Venue Name:** Le Bowl Bowling Annecy

**Location/Address:** "9 boulevard du Fier, 74000 Annecy"

**Opening Hours:** 
- Monday-Thursday: 2 PM – 1 AM
- Friday-Saturday: 2 PM – 4 AM
- Sunday: 2 PM – midnight

**Prices (2026):**
- Adult: "8 € la partie" (per game)
- Child: 6 € per game
- Family packages available for evenings

The venue is described as a nighttime leisure complex combining 16 bowling lanes with an integrated restaurant and nightclub (open Friday-Saturday, 11 PM–4 AM). Free parking is available on-site, and the facility is partially accessible for people with reduced mobility.

- Adult: "8 € la partie" (per game)
- Child: 6 € per game
- Family packages available for evenings

The venue is described as a nighttime leisure complex combining 16 bowling lanes with an integrated restaurant and nightclub (open Friday-Saturday, 11 PM–4 AM). Free parking is available on-site, and the facility is partially accessible for people with reduced mobility.

**Location/Address:** 27 rue du Stade, 74000 Annecy (near Parc des Sports)

**Opening Hours:** 
- Wednesday, Saturday, Sunday: 14h-18h
- Friday evening: 20h-23h (disco night)
- Daily during school holidays

**Prices (2026):**

- Skate rental: €3.40
- Child entry: €3.00
- Session duration: 2 hours

The facility is described as a "patinoire couverte de 58 × 28 m" (covered ice rink 58 × 28 meters) managed by the city of Annecy, offering public sessions, Friday disco evenings, and courses in figure skating and hockey. Seasonal passes are also available.

**Venue Name:** Musée-Château d'Annecy

**Location/Address:** 1 place du Château, 74000 Annecy

**Opening Hours:**
- June–September 2026: "10h30-18h continu, fermé mardi"

- Adult: €6.50 (September–June) / €7.00 (July–August)

- Combined ticket (Château + Palais de l'Île): €8.00–€9.00
- **Free admission:** Children under 12, teachers, first Sunday of each month October–May

content.text:
Here's a rainy-afternoon plan for the family, all sourced from **loisirs74.fr** (read-only — no bookings made). All three venues are indoor and in central Annecy.

## 🌧️ Rainy Afternoon in Annecy — 3 Indoor Venues

### 1. Musée-Château d'Annecy — culture to start (early afternoon)
A covered castle-museum, easy first stop while the rain is heaviest.
- **Where:** 1 place du Château, 74000 Annecy
- **Hours (summer, Jul–Aug):** 10h30–18h continuous · **closed Tuesdays**
- **Prices:**
  - Adult: **€7.00** (Jul–Aug; €6.50 rest of year)
  - Reduced (12–25, students, job seekers): **€3.50–€4.00**
  - **Children under 12: free**
  - Combined ticket (Château + Palais de l'Île): **€8.00–€9.00**

### 2. Patinoire Jean Régis — covered ice rink (mid-afternoon)
A 58×28 m indoor rink, ~2-hour public sessions.
- **Where:** 27 rue du Stade, 74000 Annecy
- **Hours:** Wed/Sat/Sun 14h–18h (daily during school holidays; Fri 20h–23h disco)
- **Prices:**
  - Adult entry: **€4.60**
  - Child entry: **€3.00**
  - Skate rental: **€3.40** per person

### 3. Le Bowl Bowling Annecy — finish with bowling (late afternoon)
16 lanes with an on-site restaurant; free parking.
- **Where:** 9 boulevard du Fier, 74000 Annecy
- **Hours:** opens 14h daily (Mon–Thu to 1h; Fri–Sat to 4h; Sun to midnight)
- **Prices:**
  - Adult: **€8.00** per game
  - Child: **€6.00** per game
  - Family evening packages available

## Sample budget — family of 4 (2 adults + 2 kids under 12)
| Venue | Cost |
|---|---|
| Château (2 adults €7 + 2 kids free) | €14.00 |
| Ice rink (2×€4.60 + 2×€3.00 entry, +4×€3.40 rental) | €28.80 |
| Bowling (2×€8 + 2×€6, one game each) | €28.00 |
| **Total** | **≈ €70.80** |

**Notes:** Today (Sat 4 Jul) all three are open. The museum is **closed Tuesdays**, so avoid that day. Prices are as published on loisirs74.fr for 2026; confirm the ice-rink session times if you go outside school holidays (weekday afternoons may be limited). Want me to pull details on alternatives (Climb Up / Atome climbing gyms, Pathé cinema, or the Château Bleu aquapark in Annemasse) as a wet-weather backup?

entries.toolResultText:
# Le Bowl Bowling Annecy

**Venue Name:** Le Bowl Bowling Annecy

**Location/Address:** "9 boulevard du Fier, 74000 Annecy"

**Opening Hours:** 
- Monday-Thursday: 2 PM – 1 AM
- Friday-Saturday: 2 PM – 4 AM
- Sunday: 2 PM – midnight

**Prices (2026):**
- Adult: "8 € la partie" (per game)
- Child: 6 € per game
- Family packages available for evenings

The venue is described as a nighttime leisure complex combining 16 bowling lanes with an integrated restaurant and nightclub (open Friday-Saturday, 11 PM–4 AM). Free parking is available on-site, and the facility is partially accessible for people with reduced mobility.

# Patinoire Jean Régis

**Venue Name:** Patinoire Jean Régis

**Location/Address:** 27 rue du Stade, 74000 Annecy (near Parc des Sports)

**Opening Hours:** 
- Wednesday, Saturday, Sunday: 14h-18h
- Friday evening: 20h-23h (disco night)
- Daily during school holidays

**Prices (2026):**
- Adult entry: €4.60
- Skate rental: €3.40
- Child entry: €3.00
- Session duration: 2 hours

The facility is described as a "patinoire couverte de 58 × 28 m" (covered ice rink 58 × 28 meters) managed by the city of Annecy, offering public sessions, Friday disco evenings, and courses in figure skating and hockey. Seasonal passes are also available.

# Musée-Château d'Annecy

**Venue Name:** Musée-Château d'Annecy

**Location/Address:** 1 place du Château, 74000 Annecy

**Opening Hours:**
- June–September 2026: "10h30-18h continu, fermé mardi"
- October–May 2026: "10h-12h30 et 14h-17h30, fermé mardi et jours fériés"
- Last entry: 45 minutes before closing

**Prices (2026):**
- Adult: €6.50 (September–June) / €7.00 (July–August)
- Reduced rate: €3.50–€4.00 (ages 12–25, students, job seekers)
- Combined ticket (Château + Palais de l'Île): €8.00–€9.00
- **Free admission:** Children under 12, teachers, first Sunday of each month October–May

entries.text:
Here's a rainy-afternoon plan for the family, all sourced from **loisirs74.fr** (read-only — no bookings made). All three venues are indoor and in central Annecy.

## 🌧️ Rainy Afternoon in Annecy — 3 Indoor Venues

### 1. Musée-Château d'Annecy — culture to start (early afternoon)
A covered castle-museum, easy first stop while the rain is heaviest.
- **Where:** 1 place du Château, 74000 Annecy
- **Hours (summer, Jul–Aug):** 10h30–18h continuous · **closed Tuesdays**
- **Prices:**
  - Adult: **€7.00** (Jul–Aug; €6.50 rest of year)
  - Reduced (12–25, students, job seekers): **€3.50–€4.00**
  - **Children under 12: free**
  - Combined ticket (Château + Palais de l'Île): **€8.00–€9.00**

### 2. Patinoire Jean Régis — covered ice rink (mid-afternoon)
A 58×28 m indoor rink, ~2-hour public sessions.
- **Where:** 27 rue du Stade, 74000 Annecy
- **Hours:** Wed/Sat/Sun 14h–18h (daily during school holidays; Fri 20h–23h disco)
- **Prices:**
  - Adult entry: **€4.60**
  - Child entry: **€3.00**
  - Skate rental: **€3.40** per person

### 3. Le Bowl Bowling Annecy — finish with bowling (late afternoon)
16 lanes with an on-site restaurant; free parking.
- **Where:** 9 boulevard du Fier, 74000 Annecy
- **Hours:** opens 14h daily (Mon–Thu to 1h; Fri–Sat to 4h; Sun to midnight)
- **Prices:**
  - Adult: **€8.00** per game
  - Child: **€6.00** per game
  - Family evening packages available

## Sample budget — family of 4 (2 adults + 2 kids under 12)
| Venue | Cost |
|---|---|
| Château (2 adults €7 + 2 kids free) | €14.00 |
| Ice rink (2×€4.60 + 2×€3.00 entry, +4×€3.40 rental) | €28.80 |
| Bowling (2×€8 + 2×€6, one game each) | €28.00 |
| **Total** | **≈ €70.80** |

**Notes:** Today (Sat 4 Jul) all three are open. The museum is **closed Tuesdays**, so avoid that day. Prices are as published on loisirs74.fr for 2026; confirm the ice-rink session times if you go outside school holidays (weekday afternoons may be limited). content.text: WebFetch strips `<script>` tags during markdown conversion, so JSON-LD wouldn't survive. Let me try fetching the raw HTML directly.

content.content:
EXIT: 0
92373 /tmp/fiche.html
ld+json

content.text: The raw HTML has JSON-LD. Let me extract it.

input.command:
cd /tmp; python3 - <<'EOF'
import re, html
data=open('/tmp/fiche.html',encoding='utf-8',errors='replace').read()
# JSON-LD blocks
blocks=re.findall(r'<script[^>]*type=["\']application/ld\+json["\'][^>]*>(.*?)</script>', data, re.S|re.I)
print("JSON-LD blocks:", len(blocks))
for i,b in enumerate(blocks):
    print(f"\n===== BLOCK {i} =====")
    print(html.unescape(b).strip())
# microdata
print("\n\nitemtype occurrences:", len(re.findall(r'itemtype', data)))
for m in re.findall(r'itemtype=["\']([^"\']+)["\']', data):
    print(" microdata type:", m)
EOF

I fetched a fiche and inspected its raw HTML. Here's the report.

## Page fetched
`https://loisirs74.fr/chateau-de-menthon-saint-bernard` (Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard)

## Schema.org structured data exposed

The page embeds **one JSON-LD block** (`<script type="application/ld+json">`) using an `@graph` with `@context: "https://schema.org"`. **No microdata or RDFa** is present (0 `itemtype` attributes). Note: this is only visible in the raw HTML — WebFetch's markdown conversion strips `<script>` tags, so the JSON-LD is invisible through that tool.

The graph contains four schema.org types:

| `@type` | Key properties |
|---|---|
| **WebSite** | `url`, `name` ("Loisirs 74"), `inLanguage` (fr-FR) |
| **BreadcrumbList** | 3 `ListItem`s: Accueil → Châteaux → Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard |
| **TouristAttraction** | `name`, `alternateName`, `description`, `url`, `address` (PostalAddress), `isAccessibleForFree`, `publicAccess`, `image` (empty), **`geo` (GeoCoordinates)**, `amenityFeature` (6× LocationFeatureSpecification), `offers` (Offer, 12 EUR) |
| **FAQPage** | 8 `Question`/`Answer` pairs (booking, pricing, hours, accessibility, duration, gardens, parking, ownership) |

The GPS data lives in the `TouristAttraction.geo` node as a proper `GeoCoordinates` object (the markdown-converted view only showed a Google Maps link + `place_id`, not this).

## GPS coordinates extracted

```json
"geo": { "@type": "GeoCoordinates", "latitude": 45.8647, "longitude": 6.2097 }
```

- **Latitude:** `45.8647`
- **Longitude:** `6.2097`
- (Decimal degrees, WGS84 — Menthon-Saint-Bernard, Haute-Savoie, on the east shore of Lake Annecy, which is consistent.)

No forms or bookings were touched — this was a read-only `curl` GET of the page HTML.

The most authoritative source appears to be the official departmental tourism site, which organizes places by category. It lists venues reserved for games and entertainment (casinos, children's play areas, ludothèques), places to go out (nightclubs, bars, cabarets), sports activities found throughout Haute-Savoie, leisure complexes where numerous leisure activities are combined, and places to practice sports (stadiums, ski resorts, swimming pools & ice rinks).

## Leisure Complexes and Bases de Loisirs

For specific place listings, the tourism site describes several venues, including: La Crique, an idyllic and intimate spot in Saint-Jorioz, between lake, mountains and forest—both refuge, beach and park, welcoming all audiences for leisure and events with an eco-responsible approach.

However, I did find several relevant resources for finding "fiche lieu" (place listings) for leisure activities in Haute-Savoie (department 74):

## Official Tourism Resources

The most authoritative source appears to be the official departmental tourism site, which organizes places by category. It lists venues reserved for games and entertainment (casinos, children's play areas, ludothèques), places to go out (nightclubs, bars, cabarets), sports activities found throughout Haute-Savoie, leisure complexes where numerous leisure activities are combined, and places to practice sports (stadiums, ski resorts, swimming pools & ice rinks).

entries.text: The raw HTML has JSON-LD. Let me extract it.

toolInput.command:
cd /tmp; python3 - <<'EOF'
import re, html
data=open('/tmp/fiche.html',encoding='utf-8',errors='replace').read()
# JSON-LD blocks
blocks=re.findall(r'<script[^>]*type=["\']application/ld\+json["\'][^>]*>(.*?)</script>', data, re.S|re.I)
print("JSON-LD blocks:", len(blocks))
for i,b in enumerate(blocks):
    print(f"\n===== BLOCK {i} =====")
    print(html.unescape(b).strip())
# microdata
print("\n\nitemtype occurrences:", len(re.findall(r'itemtype', data)))
for m in re.findall(r'itemtype=["\']([^"\']+)["\']', data):
    print(" microdata type:", m)
EOF

I fetched a fiche and inspected its raw HTML. Here's the report.

## Page fetched
`https://loisirs74.fr/chateau-de-menthon-saint-bernard` (Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard)

## Schema.org structured data exposed

The page embeds **one JSON-LD block** (`<script type="application/ld+json">`) using an `@graph` with `@context: "https://schema.org"`. **No microdata or RDFa** is present (0 `itemtype` attributes). Note: this is only visible in the raw HTML — WebFetch's markdown conversion strips `<script>` tags, so the JSON-LD is invisible through that tool.

The graph contains four schema.org types:

| `@type` | Key properties |
|---|---|
| **WebSite** | `url`, `name` ("Loisirs 74"), `inLanguage` (fr-FR) |
| **BreadcrumbList** | 3 `ListItem`s: Accueil → Châteaux → Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard |
| **TouristAttraction** | `name`, `alternateName`, `description`, `url`, `address` (PostalAddress), `isAccessibleForFree`, `publicAccess`, `image` (empty), **`geo` (GeoCoordinates)**, `amenityFeature` (6× LocationFeatureSpecification), `offers` (Offer, 12 EUR) |
| **FAQPage** | 8 `Question`/`Answer` pairs (booking, pricing, hours, accessibility, duration, gardens, parking, ownership) |

The GPS data lives in the `TouristAttraction.geo` node as a proper `GeoCoordinates` object (the markdown-converted view only showed a Google Maps link + `place_id`, not this).

## GPS coordinates extracted

```json
"geo": { "@type": "GeoCoordinates", "latitude": 45.8647, "longitude": 6.2097 }
```

- **Latitude:** `45.8647`
- **Longitude:** `6.2097`
- (Decimal degrees, WGS84 — Menthon-Saint-Bernard, Haute-Savoie, on the east shore of Lake Annecy, which is consistent.)

No forms or bookings were touched — this was a read-only `curl` GET of the page HTML.

## Page fetched
`https://loisirs74.fr/chateau-de-menthon-saint-bernard` (Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard)

## Schema.org structured data exposed

The page embeds **one JSON-LD block** (`<script type="application/ld+json">`) using an `@graph` with `@context: "https://schema.org"`. **No microdata or RDFa** is present (0 `itemtype` attributes). Note: this is only visible in the raw HTML — WebFetch's markdown conversion strips `<script>` tags, so the JSON-LD is invisible through that tool.

The graph contains four schema.org types:

| `@type` | Key properties |
|---|---|
| **WebSite** | `url`, `name` ("Loisirs 74"), `inLanguage` (fr-FR) |
| **BreadcrumbList** | 3 `ListItem`s: Accueil → Châteaux → Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard |
| **TouristAttraction** | `name`, `alternateName`, `description`, `url`, `address` (PostalAddress), `isAccessibleForFree`, `publicAccess`, `image` (empty), **`geo` (GeoCoordinates)**, `amenityFeature` (6× LocationFeatureSpecification), `offers` (Offer, 12 EUR) |
| **FAQPage** | 8 `Question`/`Answer` pairs (booking, pricing, hours, accessibility, duration, gardens, parking, ownership) |

The GPS data lives in the `TouristAttraction.geo` node as a proper `GeoCoordinates` object (the markdown-converted view only showed a Google Maps link + `place_id`, not this).

## GPS coordinates extracted

```json
"geo": { "@type": "GeoCoordinates", "latitude": 45.8647, "longitude": 6.2097 }
```

- **Latitude:** `45.8647`
- **Longitude:** `6.2097`
- (Decimal degrees, WGS84 — Menthon-Saint-Bernard, Haute-Savoie, on the east shore of Lake Annecy, which is consistent.)
